note: Native-review draft covering the full master corpus (474 equations). `dictation` is a test-corpus utterance for engine localization — native phrasing, faithful to the master `unicodemath` (never the English string). Grouping uses explicit ouvrante/fermante markers (parenthèse, crochet, accolade); powers use puissance / au carré / au cube; subscripts use indice; fractions use sur (binomial coefficient uses parmi). Capital letters are spelled bare (vé, erre, effe) and marked majuscule only to resolve a same-letter case clash within a formula. See [[dictation-localization-progress]].
